The Problem

The system was
not built for you.

Of the 48.5 million Americans with a substance use disorder, 85% never received treatment.

Not because they did not want help. The top barriers were stigma, cost, and not being ready to enter formal treatment.

The problem is not your willpower. Every existing option addresses what you are doing. None of them address why.

Until you change what you are running from — and what you are running toward — every approach is just a transfer. A different coping strategy. The same loop.

  • $80K Rehab Costs $10,000 to $80,000. And more than 85% still relapse within a year.
  • 6 wks Therapy A six-week waitlist, if you can afford the ongoing cost at all.
  • Forever AA Asks you to carry the label and attend meetings for the rest of your life.
  • Nothing Doing nothing Costs you everything. The gap between who you are and who you know you could be.
The Insight

You did not start using
to destroy your life.

You started because it worked. It gave you relief from something that felt unbearable. Over time, your brain learned: this is how you handle that feeling.

Almost nobody tells you this part: you did not just reach for escape. You reached for pleasure.

Your brain is not broken. It learned something. And what is learned can change.

Until you change your relationship with distress and with pleasure itself, every other approach is just a transfer. A different coping strategy. The same loop.

That is the gap Self Recovery closes.

"Recovery is not managing addiction forever. It is reorienting how you relate to pleasure, to distress, and to yourself."

↳ Dr. Daniel Hochman, Psychiatrist
How It Works

Three phases.
Your pace.

01
Understand

Learn exactly why your brain does this. Not theory. The specific moment of recognition where it clicks — why you keep doing something that is hurting you.

↳ Video lessons + guided reflection
02
Rebuild

Change how you relate to distress and to pleasure. This is the deep work. It feels like self-discovery, not clinical homework. Most people say nothing else has come close.

↳ Exercises that integrate what you learn
03
Graduate

Addiction becomes irrelevant. You leave the program. Not because you gave up. Because you are done. There is an exit. That is by design.

↳ A finish line, not a forever commitment
Your Guide
Board-Certified Psychiatrist Addiction Specialist VA + DoD Programs

Dr. Daniel Hochman

Dr. Hochman has treated addiction across private rehabs, outpatient clinics, VA programs, and the Department of Defense. He has taught addiction medicine, spoken at hospitals and treatment facilities, and built a private practice that filled up faster than he could manage.

His practice got full. People called who he knew he could help. He had to turn them away. That felt unacceptable.

"I'm sitting in my office treating one person at a time. There should be some way that someone can reach a very easy-to-access process that helps them understand everything from the beginning."

So he built Self Recovery: what he does in a one-on-one session, in a program anyone can access. Private. On your schedule. No waitlist.

What Is Included

Everything you need.
Nothing you do not.

▶
Video Lessons

Dr. Hochman walks you through each concept. Music and pacing designed to help ideas land emotionally, not just intellectually.

✎
Guided Exercises

After each lesson, you reflect, plan, and practice. This is how the learning sticks. Information alone does not change behavior.

◎
Weekly Group Sessions

Peer-facilitator led groups, included with the program. Join anonymously. Attend if and when it serves you.

🔒
Completely Private

No one knows you are here. Work through it on your own terms, on your own schedule, without a waiting room or a label.

resisting urges.
